Title: Molecular Characterization of the miR156/MsSPL Model in Regulating the Compound Leaf Development and Abiotic Stress Response in Alfalfa

Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. MsSPL08 is an SPL transcription factor in alfalfa that carries a conserved SBP domain and contains a predicted miR156 target site in its 3′-UTR.
2. MsSPL08 is broadly expressed in aboveground tissues (leaves, stems, flowers) but is low/undetectable in roots and nodules.
3. MsSPL08 expression is significantly downregulated in multifoliate (pinnate pentafoliate) alfalfa compared with trifoliate alfalfa, associating reduced MsSPL08 transcript levels with increased leaflet number.
4. MsSPL08 is strongly induced by PEG-mediated drought and NaCl salt treatments, indicating participation in abiotic stress responses.
5. The close paralog MtSPL4 in Medicago truncatula shows that loss-of-function causes increased leaflet number, increased branching, reduced plant height, and delayed flowering, supporting a role of the miR156/MsSPL08 module in regulating compound leaf development and plant architecture in legumes.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-Seq transcriptome profiling between multifoliate (pinnate pentafoliate) and trifoliate alfalfa leaves to identify DEGs including SPL genes (no accession number stated).
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Lower MsSPL08 expression is associated with multifoliate (pinnate pentafoliate) leaf phenotype in alfalfa, and MsSPL08 transcripts are induced under drought and salt stress, linking this gene to compound leaf development and abiotic stress response.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: MsSPL08 encodes an SPL (SBP-domain) transcription factor predicted to be post-transcriptionally repressed by miR156 through a complementary site in its 3′-UTR; miR156/MsSPL08 together form a regulatory module implicated in controlling leaflet number and stress-responsive gene expression.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: miR156–MsSPL transcriptional module in developmental regulation of compound leaf (leaflet number) and abiotic stress responses (drought and salt) in alfalfa.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: miR156 --(post-transcriptional repression via complementary site in 3′-UTR)--> MsSPL08.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: SPL (SQUAMOSA PROMOTER BINDING PROTEIN-LIKE) transcription factor family member containing a conserved SBP DNA-binding domain with two Zn-finger-like motifs (C2HC and C3H) and a nuclear localization signal.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: MsSPL08 is widely expressed in aboveground tissues (leaves, stems, flowers) but not detected in roots and nodules; its expression is significantly lower in multifoliate alfalfa than in trifoliate alfalfa; MsSPL08 is upregulated in roots under 20% PEG and 300 mM NaCl treatments, with strong induction after 1–4 days.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 20% PEG-6000 (osmotic/drought stress) and 300 mM NaCl (salt stress) applied to 30-day-old alfalfa seedlings for 1, 2, and 4 days.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Compound leaf (leaflet number) development and abiotic stress (drought, salt) response in alfalfa via the miR156/MsSPL module.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Compound leaf development (leaflet number; leaf pattern) and abiotic stress tolerance (drought stress, salt stress).
Plant_Genes.Favorable_Allele: Reduced MsSPL08 expression is associated with multifoliate (pinnate pentafoliate) alfalfa, a trait beneficial for increasing leaf number and potentially improving forage quality and protein yield.
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: MsSPL08 and other miR156-targeted MsSPLs show broad responsiveness to PEG- and NaCl-induced stress, with most genes being upregulated at later time points, suggesting roles in general stress adaptation in addition to leaf development.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Alfalfa backcross populations derived from multifoliate cultivar PL34HQ × trifoliate cultivar Huaiyin (BC1 and BC2) used to obtain stable multifoliate and trifoliate lines for RNA-Seq; cultivar Gannong No. 9 seedlings used for PEG and NaCl stress treatments.
Plant_Genes.Variety: PL34HQ (multifoliate parent), Huaiyin (trifoliate recurrent parent), and cultivar Gannong No. 9.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: RNA-Seq transcriptome sequencing and differential expression analysis (HISAT2, StringTie, DESeq2); genome-wide SPL identification using HMM (SBP domain) and TBLASTN; promoter cis-element prediction (PlantCARE); miR156 target-site prediction (psRNATarget); tissue expression profiling from public datasets; qRT-PCR expression analysis of miR156-target MsSPL genes under PEG and NaCl treatments.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Association of MsSPL08 with multifoliate vs. trifoliate leaf phenotype by RNA-Seq and expression comparison; qRT-PCR validation of MsSPL08 stress-responsive expression under PEG and NaCl; functional inference supported by phenotypic analysis of its highly conserved paralog MtSPL4 in Medicago truncatula Tnt1 insertion mutants.
Plant_Genes.Genetic_Population: Backcross populations BC1 and BC2 derived from multifoliate alfalfa PL34HQ × trifoliate alfalfa Huaiyin, used to generate stable multifoliate and trifoliate genetic strains for transcriptome comparison.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified as part of a genome-wide SPL gene family search in alfalfa using HMM of the SBP domain and TBLASTN homology to Arabidopsis, rice, and Medicago truncatula SPL proteins; implicated in compound leaf development via differential expression in RNA-Seq between multifoliate and trifoliate alfalfa and via paralog-based functional validation in M. truncatula.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: MsSPL08, as part of the miR156/MsSPL module, represents a promising target for breeding alfalfa with increased leaflet number and improved leaf/stem ratio, which can enhance forage protein yield and digestibility; its strong induction by drought and salt suggests that manipulating this module could also contribute to developing multifoliate, stress-tolerant alfalfa cultivars.

Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. MtSPL4 is the Medicago truncatula paralog of alfalfa MsSPL08, sharing about 93% amino-acid identity and similar gene structure.
2. A Tnt1 retrotransposon-tagged mtspl mutant line (NF10482-4, R108 background) carrying a disruption in MtSPL4 shows strong alterations in plant architecture.
3. Loss of MtSPL4 function leads to multifoliate compound leaves with variable leaflet numbers (1–7 leaflets), increased lateral branches, reduced plant height, and delayed flowering.
4. Mutant seedlings display three cotyledons and trifoliate first true leaves compared with two cotyledons and a simple true leaf in wild type, and adult leaves lose normal serrations at the leaf margin, demonstrating that MtSPL4 is required for normal compound leaf patterning and shoot development in M. truncatula.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss-of-function of MtSPL4 in Medicago truncatula causes multifoliate leaves with increased and variable leaflet number, enhanced lateral branching, reduced plant height, and delayed flowering, indicating that MtSPL4 negatively regulates leaflet number and branching while promoting stem elongation and timely flowering.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: MtSPL4 encodes an SPL (SBP-domain) transcription factor that acts as a key regulator of compound leaf morphogenesis, shoot branching, stem elongation, and flowering time; disruption of MtSPL4 alters these developmental programs.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Developmental regulation pathway controlling compound leaf formation, shoot branching, and flowering time in Medicago truncatula via SPL transcription factor MtSPL4.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: SPL (SQUAMOSA PROMOTER BINDING PROTEIN-LIKE) transcription factor containing a conserved SBP DNA-binding domain with two Zn-finger-like motifs and a nuclear localization signal, highly similar in sequence and structure to alfalfa MsSPL08.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: MtSPL4 is expressed mainly in aboveground tissues (leaves, stems, flowers), with expression profiles similar to MsSPL08, supporting a role in shoot and leaf development.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Genetic control of compound leaf development, leaflet number, shoot branching, plant height, and flowering time in Medicago truncatula using a Tnt1-tagged MtSPL4 mutant as a model for alfalfa SPL function.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Compound leaf development (leaflet number and leaflet patterning), shoot branching (tillering/branching), plant height, and flowering time.
Plant_Genes.Favorable_Allele: The mtspl (MtSPL4 loss-of-function) allele increases leaflet number and branching and reduces plant height, traits that could be favorable for creating compact, highly leafy forage-type legumes, though flowering is delayed.
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: In the MtSPL4 mutant, leaflet number per leaf varies from 1 to 7, with approximately 30.5% of leaves exhibiting four or five leaflets; qualitative increases in lateral branches and reductions in plant height and flowering time are reported.
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Mutant seedlings show three cotyledons and a trifoliate first true leaf instead of two cotyledons and a simple leaf; adult leaves lose normal serrations at the leaf margins, further indicating broad effects of MtSPL4 on leaf patterning.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Medicago truncatula mtspl mutant line NF10482-4 (Tnt1 retrotransposon-tagged MtSPL4 mutant) in ecotype R108 background, and corresponding wild-type R108 plants.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Forward genetic screening of a Tnt1 retrotransposon-tagged Medicago truncatula mutant collection to isolate the mtspl (MtSPL4) mutant; PCR-based genotyping of MtSPL4 using gene-specific and Tnt1 primers; multiple sequence alignment and gene structure comparison between MsSPL08 and MtSPL4; phenotypic characterization of mutant vs. wild-type plants for leaflet number, branching, plant height, and flowering time.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Tnt1 retrotransposon-tagged loss-of-function mutant analysis of MtSPL4 (mtspl line NF10482-4) in Medicago truncatula, with detailed comparison of leaf, shoot, and flowering phenotypes between mutant and wild-type plants.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: MtSPL4 was identified as the closest paralog of alfalfa MsSPL08 by cDNA sequence comparison among Medicago truncatula SPL genes; a mtspl mutant (NF10482-4) was isolated from a Tnt1-tagged population by forward genetic screening, and MtSPL4 disruption was confirmed by PCR using gene-specific and Tnt1 primers.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Functional characterization of MtSPL4 demonstrates that SPL genes can be used to modulate leaflet number, branching, plant height, and flowering time in legumes. This provides a mechanistic basis and model for targeting SPL homologs such as MsSPL08 to breed multifoliate, compact, high-leaf biomass forage legumes like alfalfa.
